title: Elbaf’s Children Become Targets
description: On pages 8–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, Luffy, Five Elders, Unknown person #8, Nami, Unknown person #9 as The Elbaf conflict sharpens around Loki, the God’s Knights, giant children, and the secrets of the island’s royal past.
scene: The Elbaf conflict sharpens around Loki, the God’s Knights, giant children, and the secrets of the island’s royal past.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Don't be so dismissive, straw hat!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You'd understand if you were born on Elbaf!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We all look up to Nika!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Not to defend Road, but he wasn't lying about us all sharing the same dream!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Hmmm...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Loki was the same...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I believe his motive for Harold's murder...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...was his attempt to follow in Nika's footsteps...

dialogue.speaker: Five Elders
dialogue.text: Nothing can justify what he did, Elder!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Hmm... LOL...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: So he's a good guy?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Not way! In hell!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He's the accused Prince.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There was a prophet's before Loxi was born that foretold that King Harold would be Stalin by his son.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Then shortly after his birth, the Royal Steel "The Horse of Fortune" Wed!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That was the same year that Linlin murdered the legendary hero, JORUL!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And the tragedies kept coming... a year later, the queen herself passed on.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The princeling would go on to use his natural strength to make the realm of the dead his own personal playground.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He killed many of those ferocious beasts and tamed the rest, he even released a few into the villages!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And that was all by the age of 10!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: He was an Arsonist!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: LOKi's mischief endangered countless lives!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He had the authority of a prince and a malicious personality... He caused hell whenever the King wasn't looking...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Then finally...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: In that very castle, Loki killed King Harold!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The greatest monarch Elbaf ever knew!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A splendid wise man who tried to connect with the outside world...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: To get people to see beyond Elbaf's feared reputation as a "land of Warriors!!"

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Lord Jarul was stabbed trying to stop lok!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: My helmet saved me in the end.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: It didn't save anything!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: LOKi's reign of Terror finally came to an end six years ago. He was running amok at sea when red-hair captured him!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Shanks?!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Shanks really came here?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yeah, in fact he was just here earlier... I'm not sure where he went off to though...

summary: This part advances the Loki and the Holy Knights through 35 cleaned dialogue beats, emphasizing the core conflict, character stakes, and transition into the next movement of the story.
